item.title: Opposition parties to pressure Ford government to reverse OSAP changes as legislature resumes
MPPs return to Queen’s Park today for the first time in 102 days. Here is what you can expect
item.description: MPPs are returning to the legislature at Queen’s Park this week for the first time since rising for a winter break from the chamber in December. Here’s some of what to expect in the next few weeks.

item.title: Canadians face ‘tsunami’ of transnational repression in coming years, cyber-research group says
item.description: Think tank cites rise of U.S. authoritarianism, AI and Carney’s disinterest in human rights

item.title: Dual citizens weigh Trump, taxes in decision to renounce U.S. citizenship
item.description: Every year, thousands of dual citizens living abroad choose to formally renounce their U.S. citizenship. They say Trump is a major factor in their decision — but not the only one.

item.title: Canada's border agency under fire for treatment of sexual harassment cases
item.description: The House of Commons public safety committee is planning an inquiry this spring to examine "systemic discrimination and organizational culture" within Canada's border agency.

item.title: What to expect when Doug Ford brings the legislature back on Monday
GFL Environmental executive’s home near Cedarvale Park damaged in shooting
The cost of this city project has more than tripled — and it’s now more than five years overdue. One Toronto councillor wants answers
item.description: The controversial drop-in centre for homeless women is due to open in the former headquarters for Craig and Marc Kielburger's Free the Childre…

item.title: What flags should fly at government buildings? Two Toronto councillors have clashing visions
Why three byelections on April 13 could change the makeup of the House of Commons
Budget watchdog nominee Annette Ryan set to testify at finance committee
item.description: OTTAWA - Ottawa's choice for the next federal fiscal watchdog is set to face questions from MPs before she can be confirmed as the next parlia…

item.title: MPs back in House of Commons today as auditor general delivers reports
Queen's Park resumes sitting after 14-week break, straight into budget season
item.description: Ontario’s legislature is set to resume sitting Monday after a 14-week break that ended in a veritable deluge of news, partial proposals and headline-grabbing musings from Premier Doug Ford and his government.

item.title: Toronto Region Board of Trade offers road map to tackle city’s traffic problem
item.description: Toronto was ranked second in Canada for worst traffic in 2025, with an estimated four days of time lost due to traffic for commuters.

item.title: How Donald Trump is fuelling the hopes — and fears — of Canada’s generation Z for their country’s future
Clashing visions define pivotal NDP leadership race as party prepares to rebuild
item.description: A week from now, New Democrats will make their choice for a new leader.

item.title: Mild weather, spring showers prompt water safety warning in Toronto
Public servant ‘scared’ to retire due to problems with Phoenix pay system
Conservative campaign manager shrugs off polls showing Liberals have significant lead
item.description: The Conservative Party's campaign manager is shrugging off polls showing the Liberals hold a double-digit lead over the Tories — while Pierre Poilievre says he's broadening his image but won't change who he is.

item.title: Here are the five candidates competing to lead the federal NDP
What’s the future of virtual health care in Ontario? Despite funding cutback, it may have taken root
Toronto police launch school zone parking enforcement program after March break
Canadians abroad call for increased voter turnout ahead of by-elections
item.description: Canadians living overseas argue barriers to casting a ballot could be affecting election results

item.title: MPs to question federal budget watchdog nominee as role remains unfilled
item.description: The Office of the Parliamentary Budget Officer scrutinizes federal spending decisions and helps to cost out campaign proposals during elections.

item.title: Elizabeth Renzetti: Doug Ford’s tried everything to keep his secrets. Changing the law is his last refuge
Doug Ford’s crackdown on ticket scalpers draws warnings it could push prices higher
Toronto council asked to oppose Doug Ford’s push to bring jets to Billy Bishop airport: ‘It’s our waterfront’
Canadian companies could face big losses as change looms in Cuba
item.description: Canada is the second-biggest source of foreign investment in Cuba, especially in tourism and mining. But Canadian companies with major operations in Cuba, including Sunwing and Sherritt International, now face unprecedented risks to their operations as the economy grinds to a halt and political change is in the air.

item.title: They were injured in military service. Canada promised better rehab. Here’s why these veterans are still in pain
PBO nominee Annette Ryan enters spotlight at a pivotal time for federal finances
Federal departments and agencies to cut 12,000 full-time equivalent positions over three years
item.description: Expected cuts include 1,793 jobs at Public Services and Procurement Canada, 900 at Statistics Canada and 942 at Health Canada

item.title: Federal government announces safe sport funding
Will Doug Ford’s ticket resale price cap put an end to $6,000 Taylor Swift seats? Experts are divided
Toronto councillor violated code of conduct in heated video exchange with head of IntegrityTO, watchdog finds
item.description: In a report that's up at council next week, the city's integrity commissioner found Coun. Chris Moise violated council's code of conduct but r…

item.title: ANALYSIS: Carney promised spending cuts — but not all cuts are equal
item.description: Finance Minister François-Philippe Champagne says the government has found ways to cut spending by $60 billion but cautions that published spending cuts require important context.

item.title: Most NDP leadership candidates say they’re in no hurry to become MPs
item.description: Avi Lewis said if he is chosen as the new NDP leader next week, his main priority will be to prepare the party's grassroots for an election.

item.title: Fast track of mining, energy projects must include plans to protect women, says adviser
item.description: Ottawa’s chief adviser on human trafficking says exploitation of girls and women is a concern for Indigenous communities near sites that employ large numbers of transient male workers

item.title: Carney and Poilievre find rare common ground on the global stage
item.description: They may be bitter rivals in the House of Commons, but outside the chamber, recent events may have led to Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre and Prime Minister Mark Carney forging a bond.

item.title: Carney meets world: Is prime minister a more international job now?
item.description: In total, Mark Carney spent 84 days either travelling or visiting other countries in his first year as prime minister. According to records provided by Global Affairs Canada, Justin Trudeau's first year included 63 days of travel. For Stephen Harper, the total was 34 days.

item.title: NDP leadership hopefuls make final push ahead of convention vote
item.description: With one week until New Democrats choose a new federal leader, the front-runner in the race says he's preparing a transition team and hopes the party can use his campaign machinery in the next election.

item.title: Europe is eyeing France's nuclear shield. Should Canada follow?
item.description: As European allies quietly explore France's nuclear umbrella amid doubts about U.S. reliability, Canada faces an uncomfortable question: join or stay clear. While Ottawa rejects going nuclear, extending French deterrence to North America risks inflaming tensions with Washington — and raises stark questions about who would defend whom in a crisis.

item.title: As World Cup nears, Toronto mayor moves to block U.S. ICE presence
item.description: Toronto Mayor Olivia Chow is asking city council to oppose any role for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement in Toronto during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, arguing the agency’s presence could “create fear” among visitors.

item.title: Canadian military personnel leave Iraq with NATO allies amid Iran war
Ont. ticket price cap hard to enforce, could send fans to informal markets: experts
item.description: TORONTO - The Ontario government's push to cap resale ticket prices for events across the province might not be much help to fans.

item.title: Canadian Armed Forces members among NATO troops pulled out of Iraq
item.description: Canadian military members and civilians are among the personnel NATO has pulled out of Iraq as the country faces retaliatory attacks from Iran along with other Gulf countries during the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Politics Insider: Attack on Jewish community a ‘realistic possibility,’ report says
item.description: Integrated Threat Assessment Centre says threat to Jewish public officials remains unlikely

item.title: Olivia Chow wants to ban ICE agents during FIFA World Cup in Toronto
Doug Ford wants to outlaw resale tickets that cost more than the original price
Ontario ‘actively exploring’ stricter e-bike, e-scooter rules, says transportation minister
item.description: At a press conference Friday, Prabmeet Sarkaria said the government has to ensure the rules keep up with quickly evolving technology.

item.title: The Globe and Mail leads in National Newspaper Awards nominations
item.description: Senior international correspondent Mark MacKinnon, Calgary reporter Carrie Tait and health reporter Alanna Smith are nominated in multiple categories

item.title: A ‘lone actor’ attack on Jewish Canadians is ‘realistic’ risk: report
item.description: Canada’s Integrated Threat Assessment Centre says the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran has heightened an already present threat against Jewish Canadians and public officials.

item.title: ‘Your days of ripping people off are done:’ Ontario to ban resale of tickets for more than face value
item.description: Ontario is moving to outlaw the resale of tickets for more than their original value ahead of FIFA World Cup games in Toronto that are expected to carry significant demand.

item.title: National Defence confirms Canadians on exchange with U.S. not deployed to Gulf
item.description: The Department of National Defence confirms nearly three dozen Canadian military members on exchange with a division of the U.S. Air Force have not deployed to the Persian Gulf region.

item.title: Randy Hillier's convoy case back on after stay of charges overturned
item.description: The criminal case against former Ontario politician Randy Hillier over his participation in 2022's convoy protest in Ottawa is back on after the province's top court overturned a decision to stay the charges.

item.title: Quebec premier dismisses Doug Ford’s concerns over province’s EV targets
item.description: Quebec Premier François Legault is dismissing his Ontario counterpart’s concerns over Quebec’s electric vehicle mandate.

item.title: 3 Quebec maritime trainees return home after being stranded aboard ship in the Persian Gulf
item.description: A Quebec-based shipping company says three Canadians have returned home after being stuck aboard their vessels in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Toronto councillor proposes banning flying foreign flags at City Hall
item.description: A Toronto councillor wants to “eliminate” flying the flags of other countries at City Hall.

item.title: Pierre Poilievre and Joe Rogan talk a little bit about politics and so much about kettlebells
item.description: In between extended meditations on all things bro, we learned some interesting things about Mr. Poilievre

item.title: Ontario to miss federal deadline for publicly funding nurse practitioners
item.description: Ontario will not have a policy in place to publicly fund all medically necessary services from nurse practitioners by April 1, as ordered by the federal government, leaving some patients paying out of pocket for primary care.
